channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Why File Paths Are Not Enough for AI Knowledge
item.description: Your docs may look organized to humans and still be structurally unreliable for AI. When humans navigate documentation, file paths often feel sufficient. We can infer a lot from folder names. We...

item.description: I have spent a good portion of my career dealing with structured data — member records at SACCOs, trade logs at a securities brokerage, and KYC verification tables on payments platforms. What all...

item.title: Google Adds Rust-Based DNS Parser into Pixel 10 Modem to Enhance Security
item.description: Google has announced the integration of a Rust-based Domain Name System (DNS) parser into the modem firmware as part of its ongoing efforts to beef up the security of Pixel devices and push...

item.title: Complete Guide to System Testing: Types, Workflow, Tools & Examples
item.description: There are multiple types of software testing that cover specific areas of your app. Functional testing verifies the features, security testing ensures your app stays protected, and performance...

item.title: UOKiK Scraper: Polish Banned Contract Clauses
item.description: TL;DR UOKiK (Urzad Ochrony Konkurencji i Konsumentow) maintains a registry of 7,500+ contract clauses that Polish courts have ruled abusive If your business uses standard contracts in Poland, you...

item.title: Has Google’s AI watermarking system been reverse-engineered?
item.description: A software developer claims to have reverse-engineered Google DeepMind's SynthID system, showing how AI watermarks can be stripped from generated images or manually inserted into other works. A...

item.title: Stop Treating Credential Generation as an Auditor Scramble
item.description: How to bake compliance evidence into the process before your next SOC2 or HIPAA audit. The pattern shows up the same way every time. Audit prep starts and someone asks for proof that credentials...

item.title: How I Would Modernize Ride-Share with AI, Blockchain & Automation
item.description: Ride-share is one of the most data-intensive industries on the planet. Every minute, millions of location pings, fare calculations, and driver-passenger matches happen in real time. And yet, the...

item.title: We open-sourced Omega Walls: a stateful runtime defense for RAG and AI agents
item.description: Most prompt-injection defenses still think in single turns. But many real agent failures do not happen in one prompt. They build across retrieved documents, memory carry-over, tool outputs, and...

item.title: Google Workspace Developer News: March 2026 Updates
item.description: Episode 29: Welcome to the Google Workspace Developer News! Find out what's new on the Google Workspace Platform. 0:00 Intro 0:11 Update on secondary calendar lifecycle changes and a new API:...

item.title: Anbernic’s new handheld is a pocket-friendly Android device with a swiveling screen
item.description: Anbernic, one of the most prolific makers of handheld consoles and emulators, isn't afraid to experiment with creative designs. After debuting a wireless controller with a heart rate monitor...

item.title: Ben McKenzie vs. crypto
item.description: A few years ago, during the absolute peak of the cryptocurrency craze, a somewhat surprising skeptic emerged. Most people know Ben McKenzie from his acting work on Southland or Gotham, or would...

item.title: Samsung is hiking the prices of its Galaxy phones and tablets
item.description: Samsung is raising prices across Galaxy Z Flip 7, Galaxy S25 FE, and Galaxy S25 Edge phones, as spotted earlier by Phone Arena. Though the base model of each phone is staying at the same price,...

item.title: Apple and Amazon are teaming up to challenge Starlink’s smartphone ambitions
item.description: Amazon has made a deal to buy Globalstar's low-Earth orbit satellite network for $11.57 billion, snapping up its spectrum licenses, operations, and assets to combine with its upcoming Leo internet...

item.title: The heist of iOS 26
item.description: The most consequential YouTube video of Jon Prosser's career opens on Prosser himself, in a black hoodie and transparent glasses. The backdrop is familiar to viewers of his tech news channel,...

item.title: Alienware’s new gaming monitor offers a 240Hz QD-OLED panel for just $350
item.description: Alienware has announced a new budget-friendlier QD-OLED monitor, the AW2726DM. As some of the numbers in the convoluted product name hint, it's a 27-inch QHD panel, with 2560 x 1440 resolution,...

item.title: Ecovacs’ new robovac spots and pretreats dried stains using powerful jets
item.description: Ecovacs announced a new addition to its Deebot line of autonomous cleaners with a new feature that could make the robovac better at cleaning floors in a single pass. The Deebot X12 OmniCyclone...

item.title: GoPro goes bigger and pro-er with support for Micro Four Thirds lenses
item.description: GoPro is announcing its Mission 1 cameras, a new line of video-centric action cams with 50-megapixel, 1-inch-type sensors and the company's new GP3 in-house processor. Pricing is still TBD, but...

item.title: Bloodborne is being turned into an R-rated animated film
item.description: It's not a PC port or a remake, but Sony is returning to the Gothic world of Bloodborne. There aren't many details yet - Sony says that "plot details are still being kept under wraps" - but the...

item.title: The Download: the state of AI, and protecting bears with drones
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out...

item.title: Coming soon: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: Each year we compile our 10 Breakthrough Technologies list, featuring our educated predictions for which technologies will have the biggest impact on how we live and work. This year, however, we...

item.title: How Hackers Are Thinking About AI
item.description: Interesting paper: “What hackers talk about when they talk about AI: Early-stage diffusion of a cybercrime innovation.” Abstract: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) is raising...

item.title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
item.description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

item.title: Mirax Android RAT Turns Devices into SOCKS5 Proxies, Reaching 220,000 via Meta Ads
item.description: A nascent Android remote access trojan called Mirax has been observed actively targeting Spanish-speaking countries, with campaigns reaching more than 220,000 accounts on Facebook, Instagram,...

item.title: The problem with thinking you’re part Neanderthal
item.description: You’ve probably heard some version of this idea before: that many of us have an “inner Neanderthal.” That is to say, around 45,000 years ago, when Homo sapiens first arrived in Europe, they met...

item.title: Analysis of 216M Security Findings Shows a 4x Increase In Critical Risk (2026 Report)
item.description: OX Security recently analyzed 216 million security findings across 250 organizations over a 90-day period. The primary takeaway: while raw alert volume grew by 52% year-over-year, prioritized...

item.title: Bringing people together at AI for the Economy Forum
item.description: Woman working with a large tool. On the left are the Google and MIT FutureTech logos, "AI for the Economy Forum", and "Innovation and Adaption in the New Era"
